title: Henriettea tuberculosa
intro: Henriettea tuberculosa, also known as "cipo-branco," is a flowering plant species belonging to the family Melastomataceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in the Brazilian Amazon, to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Henriettea tuberculosa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditional herbal preparations made from the plant include teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as topical applications for skin conditions.
taxonomy: Henriettea tuberculosa is a species within the family Melastomataceae. This family is a part of the order Myrtales, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. The order Myrtales is further classified within the clade Rosidae, a group of flowering plants that also includes the rose family and the nightshade family. The clade Rosidae is a part of the larger clade of flowering plants known as the angiosperms. The angiosperms are a major group of plants that also includes the grasses, the conifers, and the cycads.
